ORDER FOR EXAMINATION AS TO DEFENDANT JAMES W. JONES, ON SEP-24-2013 CONTINUED TO NOV-13-2013 AT 2:00 PM IN DEPT. 514, KEVIN S. SULLIVAN, ESQ., APPEARING FOR JUDGMENT CREDITOR. JUDGMENT DEBTOR JAMES W. JONES APPEARING ON HIS OWN BEHALF. CONTINUED BY STIPULATION. (MOTION TO SET ASIDE PENDING). PROCEEDINGS NOT REPORTED. (514)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JAMES JONES' MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT PURSUANT TO CCP 473 IS DENIED. DEFENDANT DID NOT TIMELY FILE THE MOTION WITHIN SIX MONTHS OF THE CLERK'S ENTRY OF DEFAULT ON OCTOBER 19, 2012. (SEE RUTAN V. SUMMIT SPORTS, INC. (1985) 173 CAL.APP.3D 965, 970.) EVEN IF THE MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT JUDGMENT IS TIMELY, DEFENDANT HAS NOT MET HIS BURDEN OF SHOWING THAT THE DEFAULT JUDGMENT WAS THE RESULT OF HIS INADVERTENCE OR EXCUSABLE NEGLECT. (SEE CCP 473(B).) FINALLY, TO THE EXTENT PLAINTIFF CONTENDS THAT HE WAS NOT SERVED WITH THE SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT, THE EVIDENCE DOES NOT SUPPORT THIS POSITION. THE REGISTERED PROCESS SERVER DESCRIBES IN DETAIL THE SERVICE ON PLAINTIFF ON SEPTEMBER 16, 2012 AT 1:45 P.M. (DECLARATION OF ANDY ESQUER, PARAGRAPHS 1-3). THIS IS CORROBORATED BY THE DECLARATION OF PLAINTIFF ELLEN HOKE, WHO STATES THAT ABOUT 30 MINUTES LATER, SHE RECEIVED A VOICEMAIL FROM DEFENDANT SUGGESTING THEY TRY TO "RESOLVE ISSUES BETWEEN US," AND THAT DEFENDANT DID NOT "HAVE THE TIME OR THE PATIENCE TO BE TRYING TO GO THROUGH LEGALITIES OR SOME BULLSHIT." HOKE DECL. 5. AN INFERENCE CAN BE DRAWN FROM THE HOKE DECLARATION, WHEN READ IN ITS ENTIRETY, THAT DEFENDANT HAD JUST BEEN SERVED WITH THE SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT, AS SET FORTH IN THE ESQUER DECLARATION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185
MINI MINUTES FOR NOV-20-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

Off Calendar Dec-02-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Both parties must deliver courtesy copies of the papers to Department 514, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than December 2, 2013, at 4:00 p.m. Judge: A. James Robertson II, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-20-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: KEVIN SULLIVAN, ESQ., 415.441.1052, FOR PLAINTIFF ELLEN HOKE.
JAMES JONES, DEFENDANT IN PRO PER.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: Defendant JAMES JONES' MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT pursuant to CCP 473 is denied. Defendant did not timely file the motion within six months of the clerk's entry of default on October 19, 2012. (See Rutan v. Summit Sports, Inc. (1985) 173 Cal.App.3d 965, 970.) Even if the motion to set aside default judgment is timely, Defendant has not met his burden of showing that the default judgment was the result of his inadvertence or excusable neglect. (See CCP 473(b).) Finally, to the extent Plaintiff contends that he was not served with the summons and complaint, the evidence does not support this position. The registered process server describes in detail the service on plaintiff on September 16, 2012 at 1:45 p.m. (Declaration of Andy Esquer, paragraphs 1-3). This is corroborated by the Declaration of Plaintiff Ellen Hoke, who states that about 30 minutes later, she received a voicemail from Defendant suggesting they try to "resolve issues between us," and that Defendant did not "have the time or the patience to be trying to go through legalities or some bullshit." Hoke Decl. 5. An inference can be drawn from the Hoke declaration, when read in its entirety, that Defendant had just been served with the summons and complaint, as set forth in the Esquer Declaration. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

ORDER OF EXAMINATION ISSUED
